---
title: "Your Talk Title"
subtitle: "A short, plain subtitle"
author: "Your Name<br>Your Affiliation"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, revealjs-style.scss]
    highlight-style: monokai
    slide-number: 'c/t'
    transition: fade
    navigation-mode: linear
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.05
    center: true
    auto-stretch: false
---

## Overview {.section-divider}

## What this talk covers

::: {.three-cards}
::: {.card .card-blue}
[The question]{.card-title}

- what we set out to answer
:::
::: {.card .card-amber}
[The approach]{.card-title}

- how we went about it
:::
::: {.card .card-teal}
[The takeaway]{.card-title}

- what you should remember
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
Edit this deck: each `##` heading is a new slide. Keep bullets short, and reach
for cards (above) instead of long or nested lists.
:::

## A single point that must land

::: {.highlight-box}
State the one idea for this slide here — one or two lines, no more.
:::

## By the numbers

::: {.stat-cards}
::: {.stat-card}
[42%]{.stat-number}
[Metric one]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[3.1×]{.stat-number}
[Metric two]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[$8M]{.stat-number}
[Metric three]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

## A figure

![](images/your_chart.svg)

::: {.notes}
Spoken narration for this slide goes here. Add notes on slides you will present
or narrate — they also power the tutorbot-builder skill if you later want a
self-paced, narrated version.
:::

## Thank you {.centered-statement}

your.email@example.edu
